svg.text: Recursion — Anatomy of a Recursive Function
1. Base case — terminating condition that does not recurse
2. Reduction — each recursive call must move toward the base
3. Combination — how to assemble subproblem answers into the answer here
Without 1 you loop forever; without 2 you do too; without 3 you
discard the work the recursion already did.
Recursion = induction made concrete in code.
• Tree / graph traversal — natural left-then-right or DFS structure
• Backtracking — enumerate states, prune impossible branches
• Inductive computation — factorial, Fibonacci, Tower of Hanoi
• Linguistic / parsing — grammar productions are recursive
If the problem reduces to a smaller version of itself, recurse.
